jokes.text: You were great. You were brilliant. It was a very good performance. Thanks, Louie. I'm not talking about you, ham hock. I'm talking about this little show your friends are putting on.
jokes.setup: Bobby assumes Louie is complimenting his commercial performance
jokes.punchline: Louie reveals he's mocking Bobby and praising his friends' 'performance' of supporting him
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie misdirection with perfect character voice - cruel but cleverly structured

jokes.text: What would the two of you do up in the mountains for a whole week? Mountain stuff.
jokes.setup: Louie asks a reasonable question about their mountain plans
jokes.punchline: Bobby's hilariously vague and circular non-answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice showing Bobby's complete lack of planning

jokes.text: All right. I'd like to get some money down on the elements.
jokes.setup: Bobby says 'just us men against the elements'
jokes.punchline: Louie treats it like a betting scenario, implying the elements will win
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and unexpected gambling metaphor perfectly delivered in Louie's voice

jokes.text: I want primitive. I don't want any electricity, telephones... No conveniences. I've got just the place. Where? My apartment.
jokes.setup: Bobby wants a primitive place with no conveniences
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests his own apartment fits that description
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jim's self-awareness about his living conditions

jokes.text: If it was so wonderful, why'd you leave? Uh... they traded me... for a couple of goats... and a, uh, and a Donovan album.
jokes.setup: Jim describes idyllic commune life and Alex asks why he left
jokes.punchline: Jim reveals he was literally traded away like livestock
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured reveal that's both absurd and sadly believable for Jim's character

jokes.text: I yearn for the wide-open spaces. Try looking between your ears.
jokes.setup: Bobby makes a romantic statement about yearning for open spaces
jokes.punchline: Louie suggests Bobby's head is empty/vacant
jokes.explanation: Standard insult with good timing but unoriginal concept

jokes.text: You don't understand it because you're a woman, Elaine. I have no idea why I don't understand it.
jokes.setup: Bobby makes a sexist generalization about why Elaine doesn't understand
jokes.punchline: Elaine's sarcastic response highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's wit against Bobby's obtuseness

jokes.text: It's so quiet here you can actually hear yourself think. I can't hear anything.
jokes.setup: Alex makes positive observation about the peaceful quiet
jokes.punchline: Jim's literal interpretation reveals his mental vacancy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - takes a cliche and makes it character-specific

jokes.text: Would you look at us... Looking for a refrigerator. There's no electricity here.
jokes.setup: The group is unpacking and looking for modern conveniences
jokes.punchline: Bobby realizes the absurdity of looking for a refrigerator without power
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor showing city folk out of their element

jokes.text: Without a refrigerator all the food's gonna go bad. With no food and no way out of here, we'll be dead by Wednesday. Mountain man! Mountain man! It's too late. He left. We're going to die.
jokes.setup: Bobby realizes they have no refrigeration
jokes.punchline: Immediate catastrophic thinking and panic about dying
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bobby's tendency toward dramatic overreaction

jokes.text: Isn't this a great feeling? You mean waking up in the mountains? No, getting off the damn floor.
jokes.setup: Bobby asks about a great feeling, Alex assumes it's about the mountain experience
jokes.punchline: Bobby reveals it's just about not sleeping on the floor anymore
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that undercuts romantic nature imagery with physical discomfort

jokes.text: I kept on being awakened by the growls of some big, hairy animal. There it is again. Wake up, Jim.
jokes.setup: Tony describes being kept awake by a growling animal
jokes.punchline: The 'animal' is revealed to be Jim snoring
jokes.explanation: Predictable but well-executed misdirection about Jim being the 'animal'

jokes.text: Where the hell am I? You're in the mountains. Oh, yeah? What are we, lumberjacks? No, we're cab drivers. Oh, I bet we don't do much business up here.
jokes.setup: Jim wakes up confused about where he is
jokes.punchline: His logical observation about cab driving in the mountains
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim logic - confused but still making practical observations

jokes.text: Something ate all our food! We're going to die.
jokes.setup: Bobby discovers their outdoor refrigeration failed
jokes.punchline: Immediate jump to death as inevitable outcome
jokes.explanation: Callback to Bobby's catastrophic thinking pattern, building the running gag

jokes.text: What did the pioneers do in a situation like this? They built cities and moved into them.
jokes.setup: Alex asks what pioneers would do in their situation
jokes.punchline: Bobby's literal and unhelpful answer about pioneers building civilization
jokes.explanation: Clever historical logic that's technically correct but completely useless

jokes.text: What does 'forage' mean?
jokes.setup: Jim says he's going to forage for food
jokes.punchline: Bobby doesn't know what foraging means
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Bobby's lack of outdoor knowledge

jokes.text: There ain't an animal around here. Maybe word got out that I'm around.
jokes.setup: Tony reports he couldn't find any animals to hunt
jokes.punchline: Suggests animals are avoiding the area because of his reputation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tony's tough guy self-image even when failing

jokes.text: What did you catch? See for yourself. That the bait you used? Bait? That's my catch.
jokes.setup: Alex returns from fishing and Bobby asks about his catch
jokes.punchline: The 'fish' are so small Bobby mistakes them for bait
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about tiny fish being mistaken for bait shows their desperation

jokes.text: This one really put up a fight.
jokes.setup: Alex caught tiny minnows
jokes.punchline: He sarcastically claims one of the tiny fish 'fought' him
jokes.explanation: Nice understated delivery about the absurdity of tiny fish 'fighting'

jokes.text: We're going to die. Isn't anybody going to contradict me?
jokes.setup: Bobby declares they're going to die again
jokes.punchline: He notices no one is disagreeing with his doom prediction
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Bobby recognizes his own running gag and the lack of pushback

jokes.text: Now they can take our bodies down the mountain in a toboggan.
jokes.setup: Snow starts falling, making their situation worse
jokes.punchline: Tony immediately plans their corpse removal
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny imagery. The specificity of 'toboggan' makes it more vivid and funnier.

jokes.text: Now they can take our bodies down the mountain in a toboggan.
jokes.setup: It starts snowing heavily
jokes.punchline: Bobby immediately imagines their corpses being sledded down the mountain
jokes.explanation: Dark but creative imagery showing Bobby's morbid imagination in full swing

jokes.text: I know they're blue berries. What I'm saying is, they may not be blueberries. And while all blue berries are blue not all blue berries are blueberries. I've mistaken blue berries for blueberries.
jokes.setup: Alex tries to explain that the berries might be poisonous
jokes.punchline: Increasingly confusing wordplay about blue berries vs blueberries
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that gets increasingly convoluted, showing desperation affecting thinking

jokes.text: Cabin fever.
jokes.setup: Alex's increasingly confused berry explanation
jokes.punchline: Jim's simple diagnosis of what's happening to Alex
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - Jim being the voice of reason with maximum efficiency

jokes.text: If you would have gotten a pineapple commercial we'd be in Hawaii now... eating!
jokes.setup: The group is starving and tensions are high
jokes.punchline: Tony blames Bobby for not getting a Hawaiian commercial instead
jokes.explanation: Shows how hunger is making them blame each other for absurd reasons

jokes.text: If it's 4:00, I get to suck the fish bones again.
jokes.setup: They're rationing their meager food supplies
jokes.punchline: Tony has scheduled times to suck on fish bones for sustenance
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny image of their desperate rationing system

jokes.text: I'm not thinking about cheeseburgers. I'm not thinking about pork chops. I'm not thinking about pot roast. I'm not thinking about Twinkies.
jokes.setup: Jim says he's following Alex's advice not to think about food
jokes.punchline: He lists every food he's 'not thinking about'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim literal-mindedness creating perfect ironic failure

jokes.text: Hey, you know something? I'm full.
jokes.setup: Jim has been listing all the foods he's not thinking about
jokes.punchline: He claims to feel full from imagining food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment where his mind works in completely unexpected ways

jokes.text: Sit here and wait for dinner to walk through the door?
jokes.setup: Alex sarcastically suggests waiting for food to appear
jokes.punchline: A turkey literally walks through the door
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing where sarcasm becomes literal reality

jokes.text: It's a turkey. Ernie? Ernie's a turkey? Well, doesn't he look like an Ernie?
jokes.setup: A turkey appears and they need to identify it
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately names it Ernie and defends the choice
jokes.explanation: Pure Jim logic - immediately anthropomorphizing their potential dinner

jokes.text: I been hunting for a big ugly moose. I ain't shooting no little birdie.
jokes.setup: Tony is handed the gun to shoot the turkey
jokes.punchline: He refuses because he was expecting to hunt something more macho
jokes.explanation: Shows Tony's tough guy image preventing practical action

jokes.text: He does look like an Ernie.
jokes.setup: Alex is reluctantly preparing to shoot the turkey
jokes.punchline: Even while preparing to kill it, he agrees with Jim's naming
jokes.explanation: Callback that shows how Jim's humanization of the turkey affects even practical Alex

jokes.text: Lord, we know this bird was one of Your miracles. No easier to create than a billion stars or a single grain of sand. And if we hadn't gotten so hungry, Your faithful servant Alex here would never have snuck up on it and blown its brains out.
jokes.setup: Jim delivers a grace before their turkey dinner
jokes.punchline: The prayer becomes increasingly graphic and guilt-inducing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sustained cringe comedy that builds guilt while they're trying to eat

jokes.text: Lord, we know this bird was one of Your miracles. No easier to create... than a billion stars or a single grain of sand. It was a truly magnificent creature, Your Honor.
jokes.setup: Jim begins saying grace over their meal
jokes.punchline: His overly formal address to God as 'Your Honor'
jokes.explanation: Jim treating God like a judge is perfectly in character. Sets up the longer prayer sequence nicely.

jokes.text: And if we hadn't gotten so hungry, Your faithful servant Alex here... would never have snuck up on it and blown its brains out...
jokes.setup: Continuing the reverent prayer about the turkey's sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of Alex's hunting method in religious language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of religious reverence with brutal reality. Jim's innocent delivery makes it funnier and more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: That's mine! Save some for me!
jokes.setup: Jim's guilt-inducing prayer has made everyone feel terrible about eating
jokes.punchline: The moment the prayer ends, they immediately abandon all guilt and dive for food
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff showing hunger trumps guilt the instant the prayer ends

jokes.text: I always thought so.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if Louie has her phone number
jokes.punchline: Louie's suggestive response implies intimate knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie sexual innuendo. Good economy and character voice, though the joke type is predictable for him.

jokes.text: I always thought so.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if Louie has her number
jokes.punchline: Louie's suggestive response implying he's 'had her number' in multiple senses
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie sleazy double entendre delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: What the hell did you say to her?
jokes.setup: Elaine storms out after hearing Alex's mountain story
jokes.punchline: Alex's confusion about what he said wrong (implied: he mentioned eating 'Ernie')
jokes.explanation: Good callback ending showing Alex's obliviousness to why Elaine was upset
